name: signature-oakland-waterfront-bay-bridge-day
founder_lock_quote: signature should be the daytime scene from oakland to San Fran with the bay bridge in the back
scene_description: Photorealistic cinematic daytime scene from the Oakland waterfront — Jack London Square / Embarcadero Cove area — looking southwest across the Bay toward San Francisco. The San Francisco-Oakland Bay Bridge spans the middle ground from left to right, its eastern span and self-anchored suspension section visible in full — the bridge is Oakland's, anchoring the East Bay to the western shore. San Francisco skyline rises in the far distance: Transamerica Pyramid, Salesforce Tower, Coit Tower, and the cluster of downtown SF financial district buildings as soft silhouettes. Golden hour late-morning light — warm amber on the water, soft blue-to-amber gradient sky overhead, light filtering through high cirrus clouds. Weathered wooden pier planks in the immediate foreground with brass mooring cleats, frayed weathered ropes, gentle Bay water lapping the pier pilings. Centered on the pier foreground: a small weathered wooden crate serving as an impromptu drafting surface, holding a partially-unfurled sheet of cream-colored drafting paper with the first SkyyRose rose script drawn in rich gold ink — edges curled, paper slightly weathered, a single fresh deep-red rose laid beside it. The 4 AM origin sketch brought into daylight.

color_palette.highlight: warm amber morning light on water + gold ink on paper
color_palette.shadow: weathered wood brown, cool pier pilings

lighting.key_light: late-morning golden hour sun from upper-right (southwest)
lighting.fill_light: bay reflection warm bounce + sky ambient
lighting.rim_light: soft sun outline on bridge cables + wooden crate edges
lighting.exposure_mood: Kith editorial restraint, magazine-grid framing, soft warmth
lighting.shadows: long, soft, warm — pointing left/east from morning sun

camera.perspective: from Oakland pier (Jack London Square / Embarcadero Cove area) looking southwest toward SF
camera.angle: slight low angle from pier — eye level with the drafting crate
depth_cues: wooden pier planks + drafting crate + rose immediate foreground
Bay water + mooring rope details near-foreground
Bay Bridge spanning middle ground left-to-right
SF skyline far distance softly hazed
camera.composition: 3:4 portrait, low horizon line + sky-dominant upper third + Bay Bridge horizontal anchor at one-third
camera.atmospheric_fog: morning bay mist receding to SF skyline

skyyrose_dna: SIG canon: drew first rose at 4 AM, broke, a baby on the way. The Bay Bridge is Oakland's; the city on the other end is Frisco. The origin chapter. Foundation wardrobe, blueprints not basics. Kith editorial restraint. Gold accent on neutral palette. Gender-neutral by default.

focal_anchor.object: cream-colored drafting paper with first SkyyRose rose script hand-drawn in gold ink
focal_anchor.position: center-foreground, on weathered wooden crate, partially unfurled, edges curled
focal_anchor.detail: single fresh deep-red rose laid across paper's edge, paper slightly weathered from morning bay mist
focal_anchor.rationale: SIG canon — the 4 AM origin sketch brought into daylight, foundation made permanent

product_staging_zones.fit: displayed on or beside the drafting crate
product_staging_zones.lighting_match_directive: match warm golden hour light + soft amber shadows

product_staging_zones.fit: draped over brass mooring cleat or coil of weathered rope
laid flat on weathered wood planks

negative_prompts: no people, no human figures, no silhouettes
no Golden Gate Bridge anywhere in frame (Bay Bridge ONLY — locked canon)
no nighttime / no twilight (DAYTIME ONLY per founder lock)
no urban grit (this is the origin chapter — calm, foundational)
no SF-side perspective (Oakland-side ONLY, looking toward SF)

atmospheric.weather: clear morning, light bay mist over water
atmospheric.mood: foundation, blueprints, the origin chapter made permanent in daylight
atmospheric.sound_implied: gentle bay water lapping, distant gulls, light wind on pier
